stories.title: D11 Graduation Day — Coronado and Mitchell Ceremonies Today

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Congratulations to the Coronado HS Class of 2026 — walking the stage TODAY at 9 AM at Ed Robson Arena.

Mitchell HS follows at 3 PM. Last day of school for all D11 students: May 22. Livestream at d11.org

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
D11 graduation week continues:
• Coronado HS — today 9 AM
• Mitchell HS — today 3 PM
• Doherty HS — tomorrow
• Last day all students: May 22

Livestreams at d11.org/calendars/graduations

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM MT

It's a big day in Colorado Springs.

Coronado High School's Class of 2026 walks the stage this morning at 9:00 AM at Ed Robson Arena on the Colorado College campus. Mitchell High School follows this afternoon at 3:00 PM in the same venue. Whether you're there in person or watching from home, D11 has livestreams available at d11.org/calendars/graduations.

Here's the full picture of D11's graduation week:
- Palmer HS — May 19 (completed)
- Coronado HS — May 20, 9:00 AM MT
- Mitchell HS — May 20, 3:00 PM MT
- Doherty HS — May 21
- CIVA, Achieve, Tesla, Bijou, Odyssey — May 21
- Last day for all D11 students: Friday, May 22

For families with younger kids watching older siblings or cousins walk, it's a moment that tends to spark some big conversations at the dinner table. What does a graduation mean to your family? What does it make your kids think about their own future?

Congratulations to every graduate and every family celebrating this week.

What's your graduation week tradition?

---

article.headline: D11 Graduation Week: Coronado and Mitchell Walk Today as Class of 2026 Celebrations Continue

stories.title: CPSC Recall — YCXXKJ Baby Bath Seats (~9,000 Units) — Firm Not Responding, Discard

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
CPSC recall: ~9,000 YCXXKJ baby bath seats sold on Amazon.

Unstable — can tip over → drowning risk. Blue, gray, pink, or yellow with duck/turtle/whale toys.

Firm is NOT responding. CPSC says: DISCARD immediately.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
If your child's bath seat came from Amazon and has a duck, turtle, and whale toy set — check your purchase history.

YCXXKJ bath seats: ~9,000 recalled, drowning risk. Firm is gone. CPSC says discard.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 12:00 PM MT

Parents: please check your bathroom.

The Consumer Product Safety Commission has recalled approximately 9,000 YCXXKJ-brand baby bath seats sold on Amazon through a seller called BenTalk. These seats are unstable and can tip over during use — a drowning hazard for infants.

The seats are sold in blue, gray, pink, and yellow. They have two detachable arm restraints, four suction cups on the bottom, and three rubber toys attached: a duck, a turtle, and a whale.

What makes this recall more urgent than most: the firm that sold these seats is no longer responding to consumers. That means there's no refund process, no replacement program. The CPSC's guidance is clear: discard the product immediately. Do not continue using it.

If you're not sure whether your bath seat is one of the recalled models, the safest step is to stop using it until you can confirm. You can check the CPSC recall database at cpsc.gov/recalls and search for YCXXKJ.

Please share this with any family you know who has an infant.

Have you heard about this recall? Were you aware of the other bath seat recalls from earlier this spring?

---

article.headline: CPSC Recall: YCXXKJ Baby Bath Seats — About 9,000 Units, Drowning Risk, Discard Immediately

stories.title: COS Water Play Season Starts — Splash Pads and Spray Grounds Opening Guide

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
COS free water play opens soon:

May 26 → Panorama Park + Venezia Park (10 AM–6 PM)
June 1 → Deerfield Hills (10 AM–5 PM)

All free. Note: Julie Penrose Fountain not opening this summer.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 10:00 AM MT

Summer is about to get wet in Colorado Springs — and it's free.

Here's when the city's splash pads and spray grounds open for the 2026 season:

**May 26 — Panorama Park Splash Pad** (SE Colorado Springs)
10:00 AM – 6:00 PM daily. Free.

**May 26 — Venezia Park Watering Hole** (NE Colorado Springs)
10:00 AM – 6:00 PM daily. Free.

**June 1 — Deerfield Hills Spray Ground** (SE Colorado Springs)
10:00 AM – 5:00 PM most days; 11:30 AM – 5:00 PM Wednesdays. Free.

**Uncle Wilber Fountain at Acacia Park** (downtown)
Opens for summer, noon – 6:00 PM daily. Free.

**One notable absence:** The Julie Penrose Fountain at America the Beautiful Park will not operate this summer due to the city's ongoing budget constraints. Worth knowing before you make the drive.

All spray grounds are free to use. No registration, no reservation needed.

Which COS splash spot is your family's favorite? And has the budget news affected any of your summer plans?

---

article.headline: COS Splash Pads and Spray Grounds 2026: Where to Go, When They Open, What's Free

stories.title: PPLD Summer Adventure 2026 — Free Reading Program Starts June 2

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
D11 last day is May 22. Summer Adventure starts June 2.

Pikes Peak Library's free reading program runs June 2–July 31. Theme: Unearth a Story. 30 days = prizes. All ages. Register at ppld.org/summer

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 5:00 PM MT

We're a little over two weeks from the end of the D11 school year, and the Pikes Peak Library District has something lined up for what comes next.

Summer Adventure 2026 runs from June 2 through July 31. This year's theme is "Unearth a Story" — built around dinosaurs, paleontology, and archaeology. Participants of any age spend 30 days reading, creating, and exploring to earn prizes. The program is completely free and open to all ages, from toddlers to grandparents.

Here's why this matters: research consistently shows that kids lose a meaningful amount of reading progress over the summer months, particularly in lower-income households and in years with earlier school closings. For Colorado Springs families, D11's last day on May 22 creates a window of nearly two weeks before Summer Adventure begins. That's not a problem — it's a chance to use the library's regular programming to bridge the gap.

To register, visit ppld.org/summer. All Pikes Peak Library District branches participate.

Does your family do the summer reading program every year? What keeps the kids motivated to read when school's out?

---

article.headline: PPLD Summer Adventure 2026: Free Reading Program Starts June 2 — What COS Families Need to Know

stories.title: Territory Days This Weekend — Family Guide for Old Colorado City May 23-25

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Territory Days starts Saturday in Old Colorado City.

May 23–25 on Colorado Ave. Free. 50th anniversary. Petting zoo, live music, gold panning.

Parking: C.A.B. Garage, S. Nevada Ave, $1/hr + free shuttle.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 PM MT

Territory Days is three days away.

Colorado Springs' longest-running street festival — now in its 50th year — takes over Colorado Avenue from 23rd to 27th Street in Old Colorado City on Saturday, May 23 through Monday, May 25. Admission is free, as always.

Here's what families need to know before heading out this weekend:

**Parking tip:** The C.A.B. Garage at 130 S. Nevada Ave charges $1/hour and offers a free shuttle to Colorado Avenue. Skip the circle-around-the-block strategy and use the garage — you'll thank yourself.

**For the kids:** There's a petting zoo, toy train rides, a dedicated kids' play area, mechanical bull, and gold panning. Birds of prey demonstrations and Indigenous dancer performances give it a cultural dimension that goes beyond typical street fair fare.

**For the grown-ups:** 25+ live bands across the weekend, 180+ food and craft vendors, blacksmith demonstrations, and Wild West gunfight re-enactments. The whole thing has a genuinely old-Colorado spirit that's worth experiencing.

It's free, it's local, and it's been running for 50 years. This is one of those Colorado Springs events that tends to feel different when your kids are old enough to really take it in.

Are you heading to Territory Days this year?

---

article.headline: Territory Days 2026 Starts Saturday: Family Logistics Guide for Old Colorado City
